evals.prompt: Set up the fixture repo by running `bash evals/fixtures/cleanliness/setup.sh`, then cd into the created directory and run /polish
evals.expected_output: Report should find debug leftovers (console.log), AI slop (obvious comments, unnecessary JSDoc), and dead code (unused function, commented-out block). Should NOT flag the logger.info call. Should stop and wait for approval before fixing.
expectations: Report flags console.log on the debug line as a debug leftover
Report does NOT flag logger.info as a debug leftover
Report flags the '// increment counter' comment as AI slop
Report flags JSDoc on the internal helper as AI slop
Report flags the unused processLegacy function as dead code
Report flags the commented-out code block as dead code
Report explicitly stops and waits for user approval before making fixes
All three review agents (Cleanliness, Design, Efficiency) are launched in parallel

evals.prompt: Set up the fixture repo by running `bash evals/fixtures/design-reuse/setup.sh`, then cd into the created directory and run /polish
evals.expected_output: Report should find a reuse opportunity (hand-rolled slugify duplicating existing utility), over-engineering (single-use wrapper), and stringly-typed code (raw string where enum exists). Should search the codebase for existing utilities.
expectations: Report identifies that the hand-rolled slugify logic duplicates the existing slugify utility in utils/string.ts
Report flags the single-use fetchWithRetry wrapper as over-engineering
Report flags raw status strings where the Status enum already exists as stringly-typed code
Agent 2 (Design) searches beyond the changed files to find existing utilities
Report explicitly stops and waits for user approval before making fixes

evals.prompt: Set up the fixture repo by running `bash evals/fixtures/efficiency/setup.sh`, then cd into the created directory and run /polish
evals.expected_output: Report should find sequential awaits that could be parallel, an N+1 query pattern, and a TOCTOU anti-pattern. Should NOT flag the one-time setup function.
expectations: Report flags the sequential awaits on independent API calls and suggests Promise.all or similar
Report flags the N+1 pattern (querying details inside a loop)
Report flags the TOCTOU pattern (checking file existence before reading)
Report does NOT flag the one-time initConfig setup function for efficiency
Report explicitly stops and waits for user approval before making fixes

evals.prompt: Set up the fixture repo by running `bash evals/fixtures/mixed/setup.sh`, then cd into the created directory and run /polish
evals.expected_output: Report should find issues across all three categories: cleanliness (console.log, TODO), design (duplicated fetch wrapper), and efficiency (sequential awaits). Report should be deduplicated and grouped by category.
expectations: Report contains findings in at least two of the three categories (Cleanliness, Design, Efficiency)
Report findings are deduplicated - no issue appears twice even if multiple agents flagged it
Report includes file paths and line numbers for each finding
Report explicitly stops and waits for user approval before making fixes
Automated checks (lint/typecheck) run before the review agents

evals.prompt: Set up the fixture repo by running `bash evals/fixtures/clean/setup.sh`, then cd into the created directory and run /polish
evals.expected_output: Report should say 'Clean - no issues found' and stop. Should not suggest improvements to pre-existing code or nitpick style choices.
expectations: Report states clean or no issues found
Report does NOT flag any issues in the changed code
Report does NOT suggest improvements to pre-existing unchanged code
Automated checks (lint/typecheck) pass before review
